User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the Ortiz Clinic platform, typically a patient or public visitor, linked to an external authentication system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: The user's email address, used for communication and login (via external auth).

dateRegistered.description: Timestamp when the user registered on the platform.

lastLogin.description: Timestamp of the user's last login.

preferredBranchId.description: Reference to the user's preferred clinic branch. (Relationship: Branch 1:N User)

contactPreference.description: The user's preferred method of contact (e.g., 'email', 'phone', 'sms').

StaffMember.title: StaffMember
StaffMember.description: Represents an internal staff member of Ortiz Clinic, with associated roles and access permissions.

id.description: Unique identifier for the StaffMember entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User entity from the authentication system. (Relationship: User 1:1 StaffMember)

branchId.description: Reference to the primary Branch this staff member is assigned to. (Relationship: Branch 1:N StaffMember)

role.description: The role of the staff member (e.g., 'SuperAdmin', 'BranchManager', 'Cashier').

hireDate.description: The date when the staff member was hired.

isActive.description: Indicates if the staff member's account is currently active.

email.description: The staff member's professional email address.

Branch.title: Branch
Branch.description: Represents a physical clinic branch of Ortiz Clinic, including its location and operational details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Branch entity.

name.description: The official name of the clinic branch (e.g., 'Ortiz Clinic Makati').

address.description: The street address of the branch.

city.description: The city where the branch is located.

province.description: The province or state where the branch is located.

zipCode.description: The postal code of the branch.

phoneNumber.description: The main contact phone number for the branch.

email.description: The contact email address for the branch.

operatingHours.description: An array of strings describing the branch's operating hours (e.g., ['Mon-Fri: 9AM-6PM', 'Sat: 9AM-1PM']).

mapEmbedUrl.description: A URL for embedding a map of the branch's location (e.g., Google Maps embed link).

status.description: The operational status of the branch (e.g., 'Active', 'Closed', 'Coming Soon').

serviceIds.description: References to Services available at this branch. (Relationship: Service N:N Branch)

Service.title: Service
Service.description: Defines a medical or aesthetic service offered by Ortiz Clinic, including its details and pricing.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Service entity.

name.description: The name of the service (e.g., 'HydraFacial Deluxe').

description.description: A detailed description of the service, including benefits and procedure.

basePrice.description: The standard base price of the service.

durationMinutes.description: The estimated duration of the service in minutes.

category.description: The category the service belongs to (e.g., 'Facial', 'Laser', 'Injectables').

imageUrl.description: URL of an image representing the service.

isFeatured.description: Indicates if the service should be highlighted as a featured offering.

status.description: The current status of the service (e.g., 'Active', 'Archived').

Promo.title: Promo
Promo.description: Represents a promotional offer or package offered by Ortiz Clinic.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Promo entity.

name.description: The name of the promotion or package (e.g., 'Summer Glow Package').

description.description: A detailed description of the promotion or what the package includes.

price.description: The special price of the promo/package, if applicable.

discountPercentage.description: The percentage discount offered, if applicable.

startDate.description: The date when the promotion becomes active.

endDate.description: The date when the promotion expires.

termsAndConditions.description: Specific terms and conditions for the promotion.

imageUrl.description: URL of an image for marketing the promotion.

status.description: The current status of the promotion (e.g., 'Active', 'Expired', 'Upcoming').

serviceIds.description: References to Services included in this promo. (Relationship: Service N:N Promo)

branchIds.description: References to Branches where this promo is valid. (Relationship: Branch N:N Promo)

Inquiry.title: Inquiry
Inquiry.description: Records inquiries or leads submitted by prospective or existing patients.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Inquiry entity.

name.description: The full name of the person submitting the inquiry.

email.description: The email address of the inquirer.

phone.description: The phone number of the inquirer.

subject.description: The subject line of the inquiry.

message.description: The detailed message or question from the inquirer.

dateReceived.description: Timestamp when the inquiry was received.

status.description: The current status of the inquiry (e.g., 'New', 'Pending', 'Responded', 'Closed').

source.description: The source from which the inquiry originated (e.g., 'Website Contact Form', 'Homepage CTA').

assignedToStaffId.description: Optional reference to the StaffMember currently assigned to handle this inquiry. (Relationship: StaffMember 1:N Inquiry)

notes.description: Internal notes by staff regarding the inquiry and its resolution.

Appointment.title: Appointment
Appointment.description: Stores details about a patient's scheduled appointment at one of the clinic branches.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Appointment entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who booked the appointment. (Relationship: User 1:N Appointment)

branchId.description: Reference to the Branch where the appointment is scheduled. (Relationship: Branch 1:N Appointment)

serviceIds.description: References to Services booked for this appointment. (Relationship: Service N:N Appointment)

promoId.description: Optional reference to a Promo applied to this appointment. (Relationship: Promo 1:N Appointment)

appointmentDateTime.description: The exact date and time of the appointment.

status.description: The current status of the appointment (e.g., 'Pending', 'Confirmed', 'Completed', 'Cancelled', 'No-Show').

patientNotes.description: Notes provided by the patient during the booking process.

clinicNotes.description: Internal notes by clinic staff regarding the appointment.

bookedByStaffId.description: Optional reference to the StaffMember if the appointment was booked by staff on behalf of a user. (Relationship: StaffMember 1:N Appointment)

paymentId.description: Optional reference to the Payment associated with this appointment. (Relationship: Payment 1:1 Appointment)

Testimonial.title: Testimonial
Testimonial.description: Contains patient testimonials or reviews, used for credibility and marketing.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Testimonial entity.

authorName.description: The name of the person who gave the testimonial (can be anonymized).

authorLocation.description: The location or city of the testimonial's author.

quote.description: The actual text of the testimonial.

rating.description: The star rating given by the author (e.g., 1-5 scale).

dateSubmitted.description: Timestamp when the testimonial was submitted.

isApproved.description: Indicates if the testimonial has been reviewed and approved for public display.

featuredOnHomepage.description: Indicates if the testimonial should be prominently displayed on the homepage.

serviceIds.description: References to Services mentioned or related to the testimonial. (Relationship: Service N:N Testimonial)

Payment.title: Payment
Payment.description: Records financial transactions related to services, packages, or appointments.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Payment entity.

appointmentId.description: Reference to the Appointment this payment is for. (Relationship: Appointment 1:1 Payment)

userId.description: Reference to the User who made the payment. (Relationship: User 1:N Payment)

amount.description: The total monetary amount of the payment.

paymentMethod.description: The method used for the payment (e.g., 'Credit Card', 'Gcash', 'SpeedyPay', 'eMango').

transactionDate.description: Timestamp when the payment transaction occurred.

status.description: The current status of the payment (e.g., 'Pending', 'Completed', 'Failed', 'Refunded').

referenceNumber.description: The transaction ID or reference number from the payment gateway or internal system.

processedByStaffId.description: Optional reference to the StaffMember who processed or recorded this payment. (Relationship: StaffMember 1:N Payment)

Refund.title: Refund
Refund.description: Manages details for refund transactions issued to patients.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Refund entity.

paymentId.description: Reference to the original Payment entity that this refund is associated with. (Relationship: Payment 1:1 Refund)

userId.description: Reference to the User receiving the refund. (Relationship: User 1:N Refund)

amount.description: The monetary amount that was refunded.

reason.description: The reason provided for initiating the refund.

refundDate.description: Timestamp when the refund transaction occurred or was completed.

status.description: The current status of the refund (e.g., 'Pending', 'Completed', 'Failed').

referenceNumber.description: The transaction ID or reference number for the refund from the payment gateway or internal system.

processedByStaffId.description: Reference to the StaffMember who initiated or processed this refund. (Relationship: StaffMember 1:N Refund)

UserPackage.title: UserPackage
UserPackage.description: Tracks a specific package or promotion purchased by a user, including its usage and expiry.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserPackage entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who purchased this package. (Relationship: User 1:N UserPackage)

promoId.description: Reference to the Promo entity that represents the purchased package. (Relationship: Promo 1:N UserPackage)

purchaseDate.description: Timestamp when the package was purchased by the user.

expiryDate.description: The date when the package or promotion expires.

totalUses.description: The total number of uses, sessions, or services included in the package.

remainingUses.description: The current number of uses, sessions, or services remaining in the package.

status.description: The current status of the user's package (e.g., 'Active', 'Expired', 'Fully Redeemed', 'Suspended').

purchasePaymentId.description: Reference to the Payment entity for the initial purchase of this package. (Relationship: Payment 1:1 UserPackage)

PackageRedemption.title: PackageRedemption
PackageRedemption.description: Records individual instances of a user redeeming a service or session from their purchased package.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PackageRedemption entity.

userPackageId.description: Reference to the UserPackage entity from which this redemption is made. (Relationship: UserPackage 1:N PackageRedemption)

appointmentId.description: Reference to the specific Appointment entity during which this redemption occurred. (Relationship: Appointment 1:1 PackageRedemption)

serviceId.description: Reference to the specific Service entity that was redeemed in this instance. (Relationship: Service 1:N PackageRedemption)

branchId.description: Reference to the Branch where this redemption took place. (Relationship: Branch 1:N PackageRedemption)

redeemedByStaffId.description: Reference to the StaffMember who handled or processed this redemption. (Relationship: StaffMember 1:N PackageRedemption)

notes.description: Any specific notes related to this individual package redemption.

AuditLog.title: AuditLog
AuditLog.description: Records significant actions performed by staff members within the administrative backend for auditing purposes.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AuditLog entry.

staffMemberId.description: Reference to the StaffMember who performed the action. (Relationship: StaffMember 1:N AuditLog)

action.description: A description of the action performed (e.g., 'Created Service', 'Updated Appointment Status').

entityType.description: The type of entity that was affected by the action (e.g., 'Service', 'Appointment', 'Inquiry').

entityId.description: The ID of the specific entity instance that was affected.

timestamp.description: Timestamp when the action was performed.

details.description: Additional details about the action, potentially including 'before' and 'after' states or specific parameters (can be a JSON string).

Settings.title: Settings
Settings.description: Stores various application-wide or clinic-wide configuration settings.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Setting entity (e.g., 'general_contact_email', 'homepage_hero_text').

key.description: The unique key identifying the specific setting.

value.description: The value of the setting. Can be a simple string or a stringified JSON object for complex configurations.

description.description: A clear description of what this setting controls or represents.

lastUpdated.description: Timestamp of the last time this setting was updated.

updatedByStaffId.description: Reference to the StaffMember who last updated this setting. (Relationship: StaffMember 1:N Settings)

definition.description: Stores patient/public user profiles. Each document ID is the Firebase Auth UID. Access: Users can read/write their own profile.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching their Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Stores internal staff member profiles, their assigned role, and primary branch. CRITICAL: The `staffMemberId` MUST be equal to the staff member's Firebase Authentication UID (`request.auth.uid`) to facilitate atomic DBAC authorization lookups. Includes denormalized 'role' and 'branchId' for security rules.

params.name: staffMemberId
params.description: The unique identifier for the staff member, matching their Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Stores publicly viewable clinic branch information, including location, contact, and operating hours. Managed by SuperAdmin/HQ staff. Public read access.

params.name: branchId
params.description: The unique identifier for the clinic branch.

definition.description: Stores publicly viewable details and pricing for medical services offered. Managed by SuperAdmin/HQ/Marketing staff. Public read access.

params.name: serviceId
params.description: The unique identifier for the service.

definition.description: Stores publicly viewable details for promotions and packages. Managed by SuperAdmin/HQ/Marketing staff. Public read access.

params.name: promoId
params.description: The unique identifier for the promotion or package.

definition.description: Stores patient testimonials, publicly visible if `isApproved` is true. Managed by SuperAdmin/HQ/Marketing staff. Public read access for approved testimonials.

params.name: testimonialId
params.description: The unique identifier for the testimonial.

definition.description: Stores application-wide configuration settings. Includes denormalized `updatedByStaffId` and `updatedByStaffBranchId` (derived from the updating staff member) for audit and access control. Read by app, write by SuperAdmin/HQ staff.

params.name: settingId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific setting (e.g., 'general_contact_email').

definition.description: Records patient inquiries or leads. Managed by HQ/Marketing staff. Includes `assignedToStaffId` for task assignment.

params.name: inquiryId
params.description: The unique identifier for the inquiry.

definition.description: Records significant actions performed by staff members for auditing. Includes denormalized `staffMemberId` and `staffBranchId` (derived from the performing staff member) for authorization independence and QAPs for branch-specific reports. Read by SuperAdmin/HQ; Branch Managers can read logs for their branch.

params.name: auditLogId
params.description: The unique identifier for the audit log entry.

definition.description: Stores appointments booked by a specific user. Includes denormalized `branchId` (from the scheduled branch) and `userId` (implicit from path) for authorization independence and QAPs (e.g., appointments by branch). Users can manage their own appointments; SuperAdmin/HQ can manage all; Branch Managers can manage appointments for their branch.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who booked the appointment.

params.name: appointmentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the appointment.

definition.description: Tracks a specific package purchased by a user, including its balance and expiry. Includes denormalized `userId` (implicit from path) for authorization independence. Users can view their own packages; SuperAdmin/HQ can manage all; Branch Managers/Cashiers can manage packages relevant to their branch's redemptions.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who purchased the package.

params.name: userPackageId
params.description: The unique identifier for the purchased user package.

definition.description: Records individual instances of a user redeeming a service from their purchased package. Includes denormalized `branchId`, `redeemedByStaffId`, and `userId` (implicit from path) for authorization independence and QAPs. Users can view their own redemptions; SuperAdmin/HQ can manage all; Branch Managers/Cashiers can manage redemptions for their branch.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user whose package was redeemed.

params.name: userPackageId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent user package.

params.name: redemptionId
params.description: The unique identifier for this specific package redemption event.

definition.description: Records financial transactions. Includes denormalized `userId` (payer) and `branchId` (from associated appointment) for authorization independence and QAPs (e.g., payments by branch/status). Managed by Finance/Cashier roles; SuperAdmin/HQ can manage all; Cashiers can manage payments for their assigned branch.

params.name: paymentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the payment transaction.

definition.description: Manages details for refund transactions. Includes denormalized `userId` (recipient) and `branchId` (from associated payment/appointment) for authorization independence and QAPs (e.g., refunds by branch/status). Managed by Finance/Cashier roles; SuperAdmin/HQ can manage all; Cashiers can manage refunds for their assigned branch.

params.name: refundId
params.description: The unique identifier for the refund transaction.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for Ortiz Clinic is designed with a strong emphasis on Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and Query as a Proxy (QAP) principles, crucial for a secure, scalable, and debuggable premium operations backend. 

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**

To eliminate hierarchical authorization dependencies and complex `get()` calls in security rules, key authorization contexts are denormalized directly into the relevant documents:

1.  **Staff Roles and Branch Association:** The `/staffMembers/{staffMemberId}` collection (where `staffMemberId` is `request.auth.uid`) holds the `role` and `branchId` for each authenticated staff member. This allows security rules to perform a single, atomic self-lookup (`get(/staffMembers/request.auth.uid)`) to determine the staff's permissions and assigned branch. This avoids chaining `get()` calls across multiple documents for authorization.
2.  **Transactional Data (Appointments, Payments, Refunds, Package Redemptions):**
    *   `Appointment` documents, stored under `/users/{userId}/appointments/{appointmentId}`, explicitly include the `branchId` where the appointment is scheduled. This allows Branch Managers to access only appointments relevant to their branch without needing to `get()` the parent `User` or `Branch` document for authorization.
    *   `Payment` and `Refund` documents (in their respective top-level collections) are enhanced with a denormalized `branchId` (derived from the associated `Appointment` or `UserPackage` at creation). This is critical for Cashiers and Finance roles, allowing them to manage transactions strictly within their operational scope without complex lookups.
    *   `PackageRedemption` documents, nested under `/users/{userId}/userPackages/{userPackageId}/packageRedemptions/{redemptionId}`, include `branchId` and `redeemedByStaffId`. This ensures that Branch Managers can track redemptions at their specific branch directly within the document, and the system knows which staff member processed it.
3.  **Audit Logs:** Each `AuditLog` document in `/auditLogs/{logId}` includes the `staffBranchId` of the staff member who performed the action. This enables branch-specific auditing and reporting without requiring a `get()` call to the `StaffMember` document for each log entry.

This denormalization strategy ensures that security rules can evaluate access rights by inspecting only the `request` and `resource.data` objects, making rules simpler, more performant, and less prone to transaction/batch failures caused by read conflicts during rule evaluation.

**Query as a Proxy (QAPs) Support:**

Structural segregation and denormalization are designed to support efficient QAPs for the required dashboards and filters:

1.  **Collection Segregation:** Public-facing entities (`branches`, `services`, `promos`, `testimonials`) are in top-level collections, easily queryable by the public. Private, user-specific data (`appointments`, `userPackages`, `packageRedemptions`) are nested under `/users/{userId}`, allowing easy lookup for individual users. Admin-managed operational data (`inquiries`, `payments`, `refunds`, `auditLogs`, `settings`) are in top-level collections for centralized management and querying by authorized staff.
2.  **Branch-Specific Access:** The denormalization of `branchId` into `Appointment`, `Payment`, `Refund`, `PackageRedemption`, and `AuditLog` documents directly enables QAPs for branch managers. They can perform `list` operations (e.g., 'appointments by branch', 'payments by status for my branch', 'package redemptions for my branch') by simply querying on the `branchId` field. This aligns perfectly with Firestore's query capabilities and simplifies security rules for read access based on `request.auth.uid` and the staff member's assigned `branchId` (retrieved once).
3.  **Status-Based Filtering:** Entities like `Inquiry`, `Promo`, `Payment`, and `Refund` have a dedicated `status` field, allowing for direct filtering to power dashboards such as 'pending inquiries', 'promo conversions', 'pending payments', and 'pending refunds' with simple Firestore queries (e.g., `where('status', '==', 'Pending')`).

This robust structure facilitates clear security policies, minimizes rule complexity, and optimizes data retrieval for various user roles and dashboard requirements, laying a strong foundation for a production-grade application.
